source.title: Accident Investigation Report - LLOG Exploration Offshore, L.L.C. - AC 337

context.region: Gulf of America - Alaminos Canyon Block 337

event.incident_type: Caught Between - Personnel Pinned by Equipment
actions_taken: Assistant Driller engaged Emergency Stop system shutting off power to all rig floor equipment
AD took manual control of PRS and withdrew upper PRS arm freeing the injured person
IP was taken to ships hospital
IP flown to UTMB-Galveston for treatment
event.summary: On October 12, 2024, a Derrickman (IP) was injured onboard the Noble Valiant drilling rig during tripping operations. The IP positioned himself on the upper fingerboards, outside the designated safe observation area, without a working-at-heights permit to tie up a malfunctioning fingerboard latch. While working on the latch for approximately 10 minutes, the Pipe Racking System (PRS), operating in automatic mode, extended to retrieve a stand of pipe. The IP was caught and pinned between the PRS arm and the pipe stand. Coworkers heard his calls for help, the AD engaged the emergency stop, and the IP was freed and evacuated for medical treatment. The incident resulted from policy violations and lack of situational awareness by multiple parties.
recommendations: The Lake Jackson District has no recommendations
key_phrases: working outside designated safe observation area
caught and pinned between PRS arm and pipe stand
normal occurrence when dealing with stuck fingerboard latches
violation of Personnel Zone Management system

hazards.name: Moving Pipe Racking System with Personnel in Line of Fire
Work at Heights (110 feet above rig floor)

threats.name: Personnel entering exclusion zone during automated equipment operation
Malfunctioning fingerboard latch requiring manual intervention
Loss of situational awareness during operations

controls.name: Working-at-Heights Permit System
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized work in hazardous areas
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring authorization before work

supporting_text: the IP positioned himself over the handrail located in the derrick, outside of the designated safe-observation area, and onto the upper fingerboards without the proper work permits
the investigation also revealed there was no working from heights permit issued during this operation
use the working-at-heights permit to shut down tripping operations when personnel are working directly on the fingerboards

controls.name: Personnel Zone Management System
controls.barrier_role: Define and enforce safe zones during operations

supporting_text: at the time of the incident these zones did not incorporate areas in the derrick

multiple workers were observed, through the use of CCTV footage, on the rig floor working around moving equipment, outside of the designated safe-zones, during tripping operations. This is a violation of Noble's Personnel Zone Management system
the violation of this safe-work practice appears to be a normal occurrence when dealing with stuck fingerboard latches

controls.name: Written Policy Prohibiting Work on Fingerboards During Operations
controls.barrier_role: Establish clear rules for safe work practices

supporting_text: neither Noble nor LLOG have a written policy addressing working outside of the barricaded walkway in the derrick itself (safe observation area), including policies that specifically prohibit personnel from working directly on the fingerboards during pipe tripping operations
a Closed-Circuit Television Camera (CCTV) camera was positioned to observe movements of the upper PRS arm during pipe tripping operations
the CCTV monitor is only actively monitored during certain portions of tripping pipe, and would not have been actively monitored by either the driller or AD
none of the 5 personnel in the drill shack saw the IP on the CCTV monitors
the IP had been working on the upper fingerboards for approximately 10 minutes prior to his injury

controls.name: Visual Observation by Rig Floor Crew
controls.barrier_role: Detect personnel in hazardous areas through direct observation

supporting_text: none of the workers on the drill floor visually observe the IP on the fingerboards during tripping operations in the moments leading up to the incident

For the 10 minutes prior to the accident, the rig floor crew members either failed to look up and recognize the hazard, failed to recognize that having a worker on the fingerboards was a hazard, or failed to stop work after recognizing the hazard

controls.name: Radio Communication of Location and Activities
controls.barrier_role: Communicate hazardous work to team members

supporting_text: the AD asked, via radio, if there was a way to tie up the latch permanently in the open position
the IP responded, via radio, that he would find some rope and tie it up
the IP continued observing the status of the latches and relaying latches up/latches down to the AD
The IP was not able to relay his own distress call over the radio

controls.name: PRS Automatic Mode Interlock/Safeguards
controls.barrier_role: Prevent equipment operation when personnel present
controls.lod_basis: Engineered safety system to prevent hazardous operation

supporting_text: the PRS, operating in automatic mode, initiated a move to retrieve a stand of pipe located in the same row but behind the IP
As the PRS arms extended, the IP was caught in between the stand of pipe and the upper PRS arm and became pinned

controls.name: Fingerboard Latch Maintenance and Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Prevent equipment malfunction requiring manual intervention
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance to ensure equipment reliability

supporting_text: the third outermost latch of row 16 on the upper fingerboard was malfunctioning in the closed (latch down) position
the failure of fingerboard latches to operate properly is a known issue throughout the industry, and has caused, or contributed to, multiple incidents prior to this incident
the crew had only been experiencing malfunctions on a single fingerboard latch

controls.name: Emergency Stop System
controls.barrier_role: Stop hazardous operation when incident detected
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response system to limit consequences

supporting_text: The IP began yelling loudly for help which was heard by the coworkers on the rig floor, who immediately communicated to the AD to shut down the PRS
The AD engaged the Emergency Stop system, shutting off power to all rig floor equipment
The AD took manual control of the PRS and withdrew the upper PRS arm, freeing the IP

controls.name: Fall Protection Equipment (Tie-off)
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment as last line of defense

supporting_text: the IP appeared to be tied-off during most of this work

it was observed on the footage that he had untied himself at least once and was moving under the extending PRS arm, less than 5-feet from the edge of the upper fingerboard, which is approximately 110 feet above the rig floor
the IP was observed wearing, and intermittently using, tie off equipment

controls.name: Emergency Medical Response and Evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Limit severity of injury through rapid medical treatment
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to minimize harm after incident

supporting_text: he was assisted out of the derrick by responding rig crewmen and the medic
The IP was taken to the ships hospital while a medivac aircraft was dispatched
The IP was flown to UTMB-Galveston where he was treated for his injuries

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
